questions.stem: What is output by the code to the right?
questions.explanation: C First, the if-else chain: x > 5 is true (7 > 5), so we enter the inner if. y > 5 is false (3 > 5), so result = "B". Then, the switch: result is "B", matching case "B". It assigns result += "1" making it "B1". There's no break, so fall-through occurs to case "C", which assigns result += "2" making it "B12". The break then exits the switch.
